meta_title: For Freelancers: SaaS starter kit to Solve no portfolio (Beginner Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS starter kit and overcome no portfolio. A Beginner-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: SaaS Starter Kit to Solve No Portfolio
content.seo_description: Learn how to use a SaaS starter kit to overcome the 'no portfolio' hurdle. A beginner-level case study using Antigravity and Claude Code for fast shipping.
content.answer_block: The 'no portfolio' trap is solved by shipping a high-quality SaaS starter kit instead of waiting for clients. By utilizing Antigravity for architectural scaffolding and Claude Code for rapid implementation, you can produce a production-ready SaaS application in hours, giving you a tangible, professional asset to showcase immediately.
content.intro:
The blank screen stares back at you, a silent testament to the 'no portfolio' paradox: you need clients to build a portfolio, but you need a portfolio to get clients. It is a soul-crushing loop that keeps thousands of talented developers stuck in the tutorial phase, perpetually learning but never earning.

But the game has changed. You no longer need to build a decade of work to prove your worth. By leveraging a high-performance SaaS starter kit, you can bypass the tedious boilerplate and deliver a sophisticated, live product that screams 'senior engineer'—even if you are just starting your journey.

steps.title: Define the SaaS Architecture
steps.description: Establish the foundation. We need a robust stack (Next.js, Auth, Supabase) that signals enterprise-grade quality.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a high-performance SaaS starter kit architecture using Next.js 14, Tailwind CSS, and Supabase Auth. Include a clean folder structure and modular component design.

steps.title: Automate Database Schema
steps.description: Define your data models to support user management and subscription billing, which are the hallmarks of a real SaaS.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a Supabase SQL migration script for a SaaS platform including profiles, subscriptions, and project tracking tables with RLS policies.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run "apply the generated SQL schema to the local supabase instance"

steps.title: Build the Authentication Flow
steps.description: Speed is key. Implement social login and protected routes to ensure your starter kit feels like a professional product.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Implement Next.js middleware for route protection and create a reusable Auth component using Supabase Auth helpers.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run "generate auth components and configure middleware.ts for protected routes"

steps.title: Integrate Stripe Billing
steps.description: Adding payment processing transforms a code project into a business asset, immediately boosting your perceived value to clients.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a Stripe webhook handler for Next.js API routes that listens for checkout.session.completed and updates the user subscription status in Supabase.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run "implement stripe-webhook.ts and update the subscription service layer"

steps.title: Deployment and Documentation
steps.description: A live URL is worth a thousand lines of code. Deploy to Vercel and create a professional README to showcase your work.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Draft a professional README.md for this SaaS starter kit, highlighting the tech stack, installation steps, and key features for potential clients.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run "generate a professional readme and push to github"

pro_tips: Always include a 'demo mode' login in your starter kit so clients can explore the dashboard without creating an account.
Focus on one specific niche (e.g., SaaS for dentists) in your starter kit to show you understand business logic, not just code.
Add a 'Performance' tab to your dashboard that displays Lighthouse scores; clients love seeing green numbers.
Document your decision-making process in comments; it shows you think like a senior engineer who builds for maintainability.
Keep your repository private until you have a polished version, then open-source a stripped-down version to drive traffic to your professional site.
content.next_steps: Once deployed, build one 'vertical' feature on top of your starter kit—like an AI-powered summary tool—to prove your SaaS is functional and unique.
content.conversion_block: Stop building for free and start building for your future. With this kit, you possess the professional infrastructure that separates amateurs from high-ticket freelancers. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
